title: rubus omalus var. pumilus
intro: Rubus omalus var. pumilus, also known as the dwarf Himalayan raspberry or dwarf Himalayan bramble, is a shrub-like plant that is a variant of the Rubus omalus species, characterized by its compact growth habit and small, edible fruits. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, derived from its high content of ellagic acid and other bioactive compounds. The plant offers various health benefits, such as protecting against cancer, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, and supporting cardiovascular health, due to its rich content of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants. Rubus omalus var. pumilus can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to promote digestive health, alleviate pain and inflammation, and support immune function.
